title: The World This Week | US Supreme Court strikes down Trump’s global tariffs, tensions with Iran reach a tipping point, India-Israel elevate ties - The Indian Express
summary: The US Supreme Court recently invalidated a set of global tariffs, marking a significant shift in international trade policy. Meanwhile, geopolitical landscapes are evolving with escalating tensions involving Iran and strengthened diplomatic ties between India and Israel.

title: From Japan to Israel, elections are reshaping global politics - ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
summary: Electoral processes across various nations, from Japan to Israel, are significantly altering the international political landscape. These democratic exercises are leading to new leadership and policy directions that could have far-reaching global implications.

title: Global Cooperation Is Changing, Not Declining: World Economic Forum - Vision IAS
summary: The World Economic Forum has highlighted that the nature of global cooperation is undergoing a transformation rather than experiencing a decline. This suggests a re-evaluation of international partnerships and collaborative efforts in addressing worldwide challenges.

title: CBC to reopen Jerusalem and Beijing bureaus amid world news expansion - CBC
summary: The Canadian Broadcasting Corporation is set to re-establish its news bureaus in Jerusalem and Beijing, signaling a renewed commitment to comprehensive global reporting. This expansion aims to provide deeper insights into critical geopolitical regions and enhance international news coverage.

title: BBC World Service to launch new language offers in Hungarian and Romanian - BBC
summary: The BBC World Service is expanding its reach by introducing new language services in Hungarian and Romanian. This initiative aims to provide news and information to a broader audience in Eastern Europe, fostering greater access to international perspectives.

title: IT rules: India proposes new digital rules to regulate news and political posts on social media - BBC
summary: India is moving to implement new digital regulations aimed at controlling the content shared on social media platforms. These proposed rules specifically target news and political discussions, indicating a significant shift in online content governance.

title: MeitY secretary says every social media user posting on current affairs can come under I&B oversight - ThePrint
summary: A senior government official has stated that all social media users discussing current affairs could potentially fall under the purview of the Information and Broadcasting Ministry. This declaration signals a broad expansion of government oversight into individual online expression.

title: Knowledge Nugget | Telegram app Banned in India: How Government can block apps and social media platforms? - The Indian Express
summary: The Indian government recently banned the Telegram messaging application, demonstrating its power to restrict digital platforms. This action highlights the mechanisms available to authorities for blocking social media and other apps within the country.

title: Election Commission of India (ECI), Structure, Framework, Composition - Vajiram & Ravi
summary: This article delves into the foundational aspects of the Election Commission of India, detailing its organizational structure and operational framework. Understanding the ECI's composition is crucial for comprehending the democratic processes in the nation.

title: How the Tenth Schedule regulates political defections - The Indian Express
summary: The Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution plays a critical role in preventing political defections among elected representatives. This constitutional provision aims to ensure stability in legislative bodies by penalizing members who switch parties.

title: PM Modi Becomes India’s Longest-Serving Head of Government - GK Today
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has achieved a significant political milestone by becoming India's longest-serving head of government. This accomplishment marks a notable period of continuous leadership in the nation's political history.

title: Global Markets: Tech Earnings Loom Amid Geopolitical and Economic Challenges - Devdiscourse
summary: Global markets are keenly awaiting upcoming tech earnings reports, which are expected to provide crucial insights into the sector's performance. This comes amidst a backdrop of escalating geopolitical tensions and persistent economic uncertainties worldwide.

title: Middle East Tensions: Markets Brace for Potential Economic Fallout - Devdiscourse
summary: Financial markets are preparing for potential economic repercussions stemming from heightened tensions in the Middle East. Investors are closely monitoring the situation, anticipating how geopolitical instability might influence global oil prices, trade routes, and overall market sentiment.

title: Strong banks do not guarantee financial stability - LSE Blogs
summary: A new analysis suggests that the individual strength of banks does not automatically ensure the stability of the broader financial system. Systemic risks and interconnectedness among institutions can still pose significant threats, even when individual entities appear robust.

title: CPTPP strengthens free trade, expands global economic connectivity - Vietnam+ (VietnamPlus)
summary: The Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans-Pacific Partnership (CPTPP) is actively enhancing free trade principles and fostering greater economic integration across its member nations. This pact aims to boost connectivity and facilitate smoother commercial exchanges on a global scale.

title: 'AI boom' insulates global growth from shocks, and other finance news to know - The World Economic Forum
summary: The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence is reportedly providing a buffer for global economic growth against various external shocks. This technological advancement is seen as a key factor in maintaining resilience amidst ongoing financial and geopolitical uncertainties.

title: Debt, AI boom and economic fragilities raise global risks, BIS says - CNBC
summary: The Bank for International Settlements (BIS) warns that a combination of rising global debt, the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence, and underlying economic vulnerabilities are collectively increasing worldwide risks. This complex interplay could lead to unforeseen challenges for the global financial system.

title: MARKET_TICKER
Sensex crashes over 800 points, Nifty 50 drops below 24,000- key factors behind stock market fall explained
summary: Indian benchmark indices experienced a significant downturn today, with both Sensex and Nifty 50 recording substantial losses. Analysts attribute this market correction to a combination of global cues and domestic economic factors.

summary: Market expert Vaishali Parekh has provided her top three intraday stock picks for today's trading session. Investors are advised to consider these recommendations for potential short-term gains or losses.

title: From Gift Nifty, Iran war, Trump tariffs to Kospi rally: 10 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight
summary: Global developments, including geopolitical tensions and international trade policies, significantly influenced the Indian stock market's opening today. Investors are closely monitoring these overnight shifts to gauge their impact on domestic trading sentiment.

title: How banks are using AI in financial crime and compliance - FinTech Global
summary: Financial institutions are increasingly deploying artificial intelligence to combat sophisticated financial crimes and enhance regulatory adherence. AI-powered systems are proving instrumental in detecting fraudulent activities and streamlining compliance processes, offering a critical layer of defense.

title: AI Agents in Financial Services Market Size to Hit USD 6.54 Billion by 2035 - Precedence Research
summary: The market for AI agents within financial services is projected for substantial growth, with forecasts indicating a multi-billion dollar valuation by 2035. This expansion highlights the increasing reliance on intelligent automation and sophisticated algorithms to transform various aspects of the financial industry.

title: Fintech & AI: The Rise of Embedded Finance in Everyday Platforms - intlbm
summary: The convergence of fintech and artificial intelligence is driving the significant trend of embedded finance, integrating financial services directly into non-financial platforms. This innovation allows users to access banking, lending, and payment solutions seamlessly within their daily digital interactions, making financial transactions more intuitive.

title: Nigeria Central Bank Mandates AI Tools for AML Monitoring - Dabafinance
summary: Nigeria's Central Bank has issued a directive requiring financial institutions to adopt AI-driven tools for anti-money laundering (AML) surveillance. This mandate underscores a growing global recognition of AI's potential to bolster financial security and combat illicit financial flows more effectively.

title: The Bank of England’s approach to innovation in artificial intelligence, distributed ledger technology, and quantum computing - Bank of England
summary: The Bank of England is actively exploring and outlining its strategic stance on emerging technologies like artificial intelligence, distributed ledger technology, and quantum computing. This proactive engagement aims to understand their potential impact on financial stability and to guide future regulatory frameworks.

title: AI in FinTech in Australia: Key Benefits & Real-World Use Cases - appinventiv.com
summary: Australian fintech companies are leveraging artificial intelligence to unlock significant operational benefits and introduce innovative services across the financial sector. Real-world applications demonstrate AI's capacity to enhance customer experiences, optimize risk management, and drive efficiency within the region's financial landscape.

title: Indian clinicians trust AI more than global peers, but use it less at work - Business Standard
summary: A recent study reveals a striking paradox among Indian medical professionals, who express higher levels of trust in artificial intelligence compared to their international counterparts. Despite this confidence, the actual integration and utilization of AI tools within their daily clinical practices remain notably lower.

title: India’s AI Revolution Accelerates as Billion-Dollar Investments Reshape the Artificial Intelligence Market - Spherical Insights
summary: India is experiencing a significant surge in its artificial intelligence sector, fueled by substantial billion-dollar investments. This influx of capital is rapidly transforming the nation's AI market, positioning India as a major player in the global technological landscape.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Future of India's IT Companies - Economic and Political Weekly
summary: Artificial intelligence is poised to fundamentally redefine the operational landscape and strategic direction of India's prominent IT companies. This technological shift presents both immense opportunities for innovation and significant challenges for the future of the sector.

title: Capability in the Age of AI: India’s GCCs and the Future of White-Collar Work - orfonline.org
summary: The rise of artificial intelligence is prompting a reevaluation of capabilities within India's Global Capability Centers (GCCs), particularly concerning white-collar employment. This transformation necessitates adapting skill sets and operational models to thrive in an AI-driven professional environment.

title: India Issues 2025 AI Governance Guidelines- How It Compares to Other Global AI Acts - The National Law Review
summary: India has unveiled its 2025 AI governance guidelines, establishing a framework for the responsible development and deployment of artificial intelligence within the nation. These new regulations offer a crucial benchmark for comparison against existing global AI legislative acts, highlighting India's approach to technology oversight.

title: Much of the world seems more enthusiastic about AI than Americans, a poll finds - marketplace.org
summary: A recent international survey indicates that global populations generally exhibit greater optimism and enthusiasm regarding artificial intelligence compared to citizens in the United States. This divergence in sentiment suggests varying cultural and societal perceptions of AI's potential impact worldwide.
